name: Synthetic CASE Essays Carry Rubric-aligned Labels for Training, With Real Essays Reserved for Evaluation
statement: Yoo et al. use synthetic corrupted essays with rubric-aligned score ranges as AES training augmentation, while preserving real EFL classroom essays for validation and testing, making label provenance and train/test boundaries central to interpretation.
quantitative_note: The source reports 40,185 DREsS_CASE synthetic samples and uses weighted kappa for rubric-based AES evaluation.
caveats: CASE generation is corruption-based rather than LLM-generated student response simulation.
Synthetic samples support training augmentation, not synthetic-only validation.
The result is bounded to EFL essay scoring and DREsS rubric dimensions.
evaluation_method: Synthetic rubric-aligned training data is evaluated through downstream AES performance on real classroom essay validation/test splits.
reported_metrics: Weighted kappa and dataset composition counts.
limitations: Synthetic score labels are generated by the corruption process and should not be conflated with independent human ratings.
Does not establish that synthetic essays can replace real held-out evaluation data.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases DREsS_CASE composition, rubric-score alignment, and real-data evaluation boundaries.
